name: Pipedrive CRM
description: Drive Pipedrive (CRM) from any AI agent: search and manage deals, persons, organizations, activities, pipelines, and stages. 18 ready-to-use tools covering the most common sales workflows. API-token auth via query string, no SDK, no OAuth dance.
instructions:
This connector uses the Pipedrive REST API.

**Versions**: most resources (deals, persons, organizations, activities, pipelines, stages, users) are on the newer **v2** API (`/api/v2/...`). The universal item-search endpoint is still on v1 (`/v1/itemSearch`). The adapter mixes both as appropriate — you don't need to know the distinction, just pick the tool.

**Setup**:
1. Sign in to Pipedrive → top-right avatar → **Personal preferences → API**. Copy the **personal API token** shown there.
2. Paste it as `PIPEDRIVE_API_TOKEN`.
3. (Optional) If your account is on a custom company subdomain that requires it, set `PIPEDRIVE_COMPANY_DOMAIN` to your subdomain (e.g. `acme` if your CRM lives at `acme.pipedrive.com`). The default base URL `https://api.pipedrive.com` works for almost everyone since the token already routes to the right company.

**Authentication**: API token in query string (`?api_token=...`) — every request automatically appends it. The token belongs to a single user; everything the connector does is attributed to that user. For multi-user installs, use a dedicated service-account user with the permissions you want the agent to have.

**Pagination**: v2 endpoints use **cursor-based pagination** (`limit` + `cursor`). Each list response carries `additional_data.next_cursor` — pass it as `cursor` on the next call. `limit` defaults to 100, max 500. The adapter's list tools expose both parameters explicitly.

**Search**: for fuzzy text matching across multiple entity types, use `pipedrive_search` (universal). For type-narrowed search inside a known entity, use the per-resource `pipedrive_search_deals` / `pipedrive_search_persons`.

**Custom fields**: every CRM has custom fields. v2 endpoints accept and return them under the `custom_fields` body/response key. The shape is `{field_api_key: value}` — get the API keys from `GET /v1/dealFields` (and `personFields`, `organizationFields`). The adapter ships `pipedrive_list_deal_fields` / `pipedrive_list_person_fields` / `pipedrive_list_organization_fields` for this.

**Rate limits**: Pipedrive applies a per-token rate limit (~80 req / 2 seconds per company, plus daily quotas that scale with plan). On 429, back off — the engine does NOT auto-retry.

**Webhooks not in scope**: Pipedrive can push webhook events for deal/person changes; receiving them requires a separately-hosted endpoint and is outside this connector's read/write scope.

**Filters & sorting**: list endpoints accept `filter_id`, `owner_id`, `status` for deals; `sort` parameter accepts comma-separated field directives like `add_time DESC,update_time DESC` on v2. Check Pipedrive's API reference for the supported sort fields per resource.

**Out of scope here**: leads (v1 only), products, files, mailbox, projects, and goals — add purpose-built tools if your workflows need them.

tools.name: pipedrive_search
tools.description: Universal fuzzy search across deals, persons, organizations, products, files, mailbox messages and projects in one call. Use this when the agent only has a free-text query (a person name, deal title, company name) and doesn't know which entity type to look in. For narrower searches, prefer the per-resource search tools.

term.description: Search query. Min 2 chars normally, min 1 char if exact_match is true.

item_types.description: Comma-separated list of item types to search. Allowed: deal, person, organization, product, file, mail_attachment, project. Omit for all types.

fields.description: Comma-separated fields to search within (e.g. 'title,notes' for deals). Omit to search default fields per type.

exact_match.description: If true, return only items whose searched field contains the term as a whole word (case-insensitive). Default false (substring match).

tools.name: pipedrive_list_deals
tools.description: List deals with optional filtering by owner, person, organization, pipeline, stage, status. Supports cursor pagination. Returns the deal id, title, value, currency, stage_id, status, owner_id, person_id, org_id, add_time, update_time, expected_close_date and custom_fields for each match.

filter_id.description: Apply a saved filter by its numeric ID.

owner_id.description: Only deals owned by this user ID.

person_id.description: Only deals linked to this person ID.

org_id.description: Only deals linked to this organization ID.

pipeline_id.description: Only deals in this pipeline ID.

stage_id.description: Only deals in this stage ID.

status.description: One of: open, won, lost, deleted. Default returns all but deleted.

sort_by.description: Field to sort by, e.g. add_time, update_time, value, expected_close_date.

cursor.description: Pagination cursor from a prior response's additional_data.next_cursor.

tools.name: pipedrive_get_deal
tools.description: Fetch a single deal by its numeric ID. Returns the full deal object including custom fields and the linked person, organization and owner details.

tools.name: pipedrive_create_deal
tools.description: Create a new deal. Only `title` is required; everything else (value, currency, owner, linked person/org, stage, expected_close_date, custom fields) is optional but recommended for forecasting accuracy.

currency.description: ISO 4217 code, e.g. 'USD', 'EUR'. Defaults to the company's default currency.

owner_id.description: User ID of the deal owner. Defaults to the API token's user.

person_id.description: ID of the primary contact person on this deal.

org_id.description: ID of the organization this deal belongs to.

pipeline_id.description: Pipeline ID. Defaults to the default pipeline.

stage_id.description: Stage ID (must belong to the pipeline).

status.description: open, won, or lost. Default open.

expected_close_date.description: Expected close date in YYYY-MM-DD format.

label_ids.description: Array of label IDs to attach.

visible_to.description: Visibility level: 1 (Owner & followers), 3 (Entire company). Some plans add 5/7 for group sharing.

custom_fields.description: Object of custom field API keys to values. Discover keys via pipedrive_list_deal_fields.

tools.name: pipedrive_update_deal
tools.description: Partial-update a deal — only pass the fields you want to change. Common uses: change stage_id (move deal in pipeline), set status=won|lost (close deal), update value or expected_close_date.

stage_id.description: Move to a different stage (within the same or a different pipeline).

tools.name: pipedrive_delete_deal
tools.description: Soft-delete a deal. The deal moves to the trash and can be restored within 30 days from the Pipedrive UI. To hard-delete, the operator must purge from the UI separately.

tools.name: pipedrive_search_deals
tools.description: Narrower search than pipedrive_search — only looks inside deals (title and custom fields). Use when the agent knows it's looking for a deal specifically.

term.description: Search query (min 2 chars, min 1 if exact_match is true).

fields.description: Comma-separated fields to search (title, notes, custom_fields). Default: title, custom_fields.

person_id.description: Limit to deals linked to this person.

organization_id.description: Limit to deals linked to this organization.

tools.name: pipedrive_list_persons
tools.description: List persons (contacts) with optional filtering by owner, organization, or label. Cursor-paginated. Each person has emails[], phones[], owner_id, org_id, label_ids, marketing_status and custom_fields.

filter_id.description: Apply a saved filter by ID.

owner_id.description: Only persons owned by this user ID.

org_id.description: Only persons linked to this organization.

tools.name: pipedrive_get_person
tools.description: Fetch a single person by ID. Returns full contact details including emails, phones, linked organization, owner, label IDs, marketing_status and custom fields.

tools.name: pipedrive_create_person
tools.description: Create a new person (contact). Only `name` is required. Emails and phones are arrays of objects: `[{value:'a@b.com', primary:true, label:'work'}]`.

owner_id.description: User ID of the person's owner.

emails.description: Array of email objects: [{value:'a@b.com', primary:true, label:'work'}]

phones.description: Array of phone objects: [{value:'+15551234', primary:true, label:'work'}]

label_ids.description: Array of label IDs to attach.

marketing_status.description: Marketing opt-in status. One of: no_consent, unsubscribed, subscribed, archived.

custom_fields.description: Custom fields object (use pipedrive_list_person_fields to discover keys).

tools.name: pipedrive_update_person
tools.description: Partial-update a person. Pass only the fields to change. Emails and phones are REPLACED (not merged) if provided — to add an email, fetch current emails first, append, then send the full array.

org_id.description: Link to a different organization (or null to unlink).

tools.name: pipedrive_list_organizations
tools.description: List organizations (companies) with optional filtering by owner or saved filter. Cursor-paginated. Each org has name, address, owner_id, label_ids, custom_fields.

tools.name: pipedrive_create_organization
tools.description: Create a new organization. Only `name` is required.

address.description: Full street address as a single string.

tools.name: pipedrive_list_activities
tools.description: List activities (calls, meetings, tasks, emails, etc.) with optional filtering by user, deal, person, type or date range. Activities are the touchpoint log of a CRM and key signal for forecast.

type.description: Activity type key (call, meeting, task, email, deadline, lunch, ...). Use pipedrive_list_activity_types to discover.

done.description: true = only completed activities; false = only open ones; omit for all.

tools.name: pipedrive_create_activity
tools.description: Log an activity (call, meeting, task, email, etc.). Link to a deal/person/org for context. Setting `due_date` + `due_time` schedules it; omitting both creates an untimed task.

type.description: Activity type key (call, meeting, task, email, deadline, lunch). Required.

duration.description: Planned duration (HH:MM). Used for calendar blocking.

user_id.description: Assigned user. Defaults to the API token's user.

done.description: If true, mark as already completed.

tools.name: pipedrive_list_pipelines
tools.description: List all pipelines on this Pipedrive account. Each pipeline returns its id, name, order_nr (display order) and active flag. Pipelines are the funnels deals move through.

tools.name: pipedrive_list_stages
tools.description: List stages, optionally filtered by pipeline. Each stage has id, name, pipeline_id, order_nr, deal_probability (default win-rate %) and rotten_flag (whether stale deals turn rotten). Use the stage IDs when creating or moving deals.

pipeline_id.description: If set, only stages in this pipeline.

tools.name: pipedrive_list_users
tools.description: List users on this Pipedrive account (sales reps, admins). Use to map user IDs to names when displaying deal owners.

tools.name: pipedrive_get_current_user
tools.description: Return the user account that the API token belongs to (whoami). Useful at agent startup to know whose perspective the agent operates from.

tools.name: pipedrive_list_deal_fields
tools.description: List all deal fields including custom fields with their API keys, types, and pick-list options. Required to compose the custom_fields object for pipedrive_create_deal / pipedrive_update_deal.

tools.name: pipedrive_list_person_fields
tools.description: List all person fields including custom fields with their API keys. Required to compose the custom_fields object for pipedrive_create_person / pipedrive_update_person.

tools.name: pipedrive_list_organization_fields
tools.description: List all organization fields including custom fields with their API keys. Required to compose the custom_fields object for pipedrive_create_organization.
